challenge: Build with DataHub: The Agent Hackathon

open_first.label: Live demo
open_first.why: Fastest visual scan of the messy data, context read, approval queue, and receipt-backed action plan.

open_first.label: Demo video
open_first.why: Two-minute narrated walkthrough of the context-before-action loop.

open_first.label: Scoring brief
open_first.why: Maps each major judging claim to concrete files.

open_first.label: One-command reproduction receipt
open_first.why: Shows the generated proof chain and the command that regenerates it.

claim_snapshot.proof: The demo prepares dataset identity, schema, ownership, glossary, and lineage context before agent decisions.

claim_snapshot.claim: The agent does real workflow work.
claim_snapshot.proof: Three messy requests become one safe internal task, one approval-required task, and one blocked external outreach attempt.
Missing owners, unclear contacts, and external outreach are approval-gated or blocked before action.
The local proof chain regenerates artifacts, validates them, and builds/tests the page.

what_is_real_now: Public live demo and public GitHub repository are available.
Local scripts generate DataHub-ready dry-run metadata, Rest.li ingestProposal bodies, and MCP-style context reads.
The agent decision output, approval queue, safety policy matrix, and receipts are inspectable files.
The evidence chain is runnable without DataHub credentials, Docker, customer data, or off-platform actions.
optional_next_steps: Run the optional local DataHub GMS Rest.li ingestProposal path from the runbook when judges want live local metadata mutation.
Replace the static context packet with live DataHub MCP / Agent Context Kit reads.
Write approved workflow receipt outcomes back as DataHub-linked auditable metadata.
